title: dianthus collinus
intro: Dianthus collinus, also known as the Fringed Pink, is a perennial plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, the plant's flowers and leaves have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Dianthus collinus are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Dianthus collinus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
